description_long: This rifle features a distinctive circular drum magazine mounted horizontally above the trigger assembly, reminiscent of designs like the PPSh-41 submachine gun. The main body is constructed from dark metal, with a robust, utilitarian appearance and a matte finish. The grip is ergonomically shaped and appears to be made of a different, possibly polymer or bakelite material, providing a secure hold. The rifle includes a simple wire-frame shoulder stock, which is lightweight and functional. The barrel is relatively short and unadorned, with no visible muzzle device. Iron sights are present, with a front post and a rear notch, suitable for basic aiming in urban environments.
description: A dark metal rifle with a circular drum magazine, ergonomic grip, and wire-frame stock, featuring iron sights and a short barrel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the circular drum magazine, ergonomic grip, and wire-frame stock clearly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the round drum magazine and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the drum magazine and the overall compactness of the rifle.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the circular shape of the drum magazine and the linear alignment of the barrel and sights.
